title: Farm Ballads
summaries: "Farm Ballads" by Will Carleton is a collection of poems written during the late 19th century. This book reflects the life and experiences of rural America, capturing the essence of farm life through the lens of humor, love, heartache, and social commentary. It offers insights into the complexities of relationships, both romantic and familial, within the backdrop of rural living.  The collection features various poems that explore themes such as marital discord in "Betsey and I Are Out" and "How Betsey and I Made Up," showcasing the ups and downs of relationships with whimsy and earnestness. Other poems, like "Gone with a Handsomer Man," tackle themes of love and loss in a lighthearted yet poignant manner. Carleton's verse walks readers through the triumphs and trials of everyday life on the farm, from raising children to coping with economic challenges. With its blend of humor, nostalgia, and realism, "Farm Ballads" provides a relatable glimpse into the heart of rural life and the human spirit's resilience.
